docs.title: OmniGraph
docs.text:
OmniGraph helps Unreal teams generate clean, high-resolution graph images for documentation, tutorials, reviews, and internal knowledge bases without viewport clutter.

Instead of manual screenshot workflows, OmniGraph captures graph content directly from editor graph widgets, producing consistent output that is ready for professional documentation.

docs.title: Value Proposition
docs.text:
• Capture graph visuals at documentation quality with consistent framing.
  • Avoid editor viewport noise and ad hoc screenshot cleanup.
  • Standardize deliverables across technical artists, designers, and engineers.
  • Scale from one-off exports to repeatable batch documentation workflows.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Batch processing: Export multiple supported assets in a single run.
  • High-resolution support: Tune capture scale and limits for publication-ready outputs.
  • Clean presentation controls: Configure background/grid behavior for clearer results in docs.
  • Editor integration: Launch exports from the Content Browser and manage defaults in plugin settings.

docs.title: Quick Start Workflow
docs.text:
1. Enable OmniGraph in Unreal Editor.
  2. Open Project Settings -> Plugins -> OmniGraph and configure output defaults.
  3. Select supported assets in the Content Browser.
  4. Right-click and run the graph export action.
  5. Use the generated PNG files in Marketplace/Fab pages, manuals, and release notes.

Info

OmniGraph is designed for teams that need reliable graph visuals for external-facing documentation and internal production references.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
This guide covers enabling OmniGraph, finding the export workflow in Unreal Editor, and applying initial configuration for consistent results.

Technical Requirements

  • Unreal Engine 5.0.0 or newer.
  • Editor workflow only (not intended for runtime/gameplay use).
  • Project-level access to Project Settings -> Plugins -> OmniGraph.

docs.title: Enable OmniGraph
docs.text:
1. Place the plugin under your project at Plugins/OmniGraph.
  2. Open the Unreal project in the editor.
  3. Go to Edit -> Plugins and search for OmniGraph.
  4. Enable the plugin if it is not already active.
  5. Restart the editor when prompted.

Warning

OmniGraph is designed for editor-based content capture and documentation workflows.

docs.title: Find the tool in Unreal Editor
docs.text:
1. Open Project Settings -> Plugins -> OmniGraph to configure defaults.
  2. In the Content Browser, select one or more supported graph assets.
  3. Right-click the selection and run the graph export action.
  4. Choose the output destination for generated PNG files.

docs.title: Initial configuration checklist
docs.text:
Before your first production export, configure:

  1. Default output directory.
  2. Resolution scale and dimension limits for target documentation quality.
  3. Bounds/padding for consistent framing.
  4. Background/grid behavior to match your documentation style.

docs.title: Workflow summary
docs.text:
1. Configure defaults once in plugin settings.
  2. Select assets in the Content Browser.
  3. Launch export from the right-click menu.
  4. Reuse outputs in Marketplace/Fab pages, manuals, and team documentation.
